Bio:
Anjali Nigam is the Attorney and Owner of The Nigam Law Firm, located in Houston, Texas. Licensed to practice in Texas since 2001, Ms. Nigam earned her Juris Doctor from the University of Houston Law Center. She has trained at some of the best litigation firms in Texas, gaining extensive experience litigating cases on both sides of the aisle in state and federal courts nationwide. Her well-rounded background includes handling personal injury, commercial, class action, and antitrust matters, providing her with valuable insight into defense strategies that now benefits her plaintiff clients.

As the founding attorney of The Nigam Law Firm, Ms. Nigam focuses on representing clients who have experienced traumatic and life-changing events, including personal injury cases, sexual abuse and trauma, and human trafficking. A recognized leader in her field, she has been selected to Super Lawyers from 2017 to 2025 for her work in personal injury law. Known as a tenacious fighter, Ms. Nigam is a frequent speaker on trial law topics and has presented at events hosted by the Houston Trial Lawyers Association, State Bar of Texas, and University of Texas Law School. Her dedication to clients and aggressive advocacy set her apart in providing personalized representation for those who have been hurt.

Bio:
Yasmin Voglewede is the Attorney and Owner of Elvora Law Firm in San Antonio, Texas, where she focuses her practice on immigration law. With over a decade of experience in the field, she began her career as a BIA (Board of Immigration Appeals) Fully Accredited Representative before becoming a licensed attorney. She previously worked with Catholic Charities, Archdiocese of San Antonio, practicing family-based immigration and asylum law. Her practice areas encompass a comprehensive range of immigration matters including asylum, deportation defense, family visas, green cards, citizenship, immigration appeals, and various visa categories.

Voglewede earned her J.D. from St. Mary's University School of Law in 2003 and her B.A. from the University of Notre Dame in 2000. She is licensed to practice in both Texas and New Mexico and is fluent in English and Spanish. A member of the American Immigration Lawyers Association (AILA), she has made notable contributions to immigration law, including her work on the 2005 case Mohammed v. Gonzales, which helped expand women's ability to cite female genital mutilation as grounds for asylum claims.

Bio:
Veronica Valenzuela is the Attorney and Owner of V.Valenzuela Law Firm, PLLC, a personal injury law firm based in Austin, Texas. As a first-generation Mexican-American and bilingual attorney, she brings over 20 years of litigation experience to her practice, specializing in car accidents, truck accidents, motorcycle collisions, and other personal injury matters. Licensed by the State Bar of Texas, Veronica earned her Juris Doctor from St. Mary's University and her B.A. in Criminal Justice from the University of Texas - San Antonio.

Throughout her career, Veronica has recovered millions of dollars in settlements for personal injury clients across Texas. After initially opening her firm in 2008 and gaining diverse experience in multiple areas of law including commercial litigation, family law, and criminal defense, she later joined a high-volume personal injury firm before reopening V.Valenzuela Law Firm in 2023 with a renewed focus on quality client representation. She is an active member of the Austin Bar Association, Travis County Women Lawyers Association, and the Texas State Bar's Women and the Law Section and Hispanic Issues Section. Beyond her legal practice, Veronica is committed to community service, volunteering as a mentor for the Seedling Program for children with incarcerated parents and serving at ReStore Austin.

Bio:
Alexander J. Houthuijzen, known as "Alex the Defender," is the Owner and Attorney at Alexander J. Houthuijzen, Attorney-At-Law, PLLC, a criminal defense firm located in downtown Houston, Texas. Licensed to practice in Texas since November 2016, Alex graduated from South Texas College of Law Houston in May 2013, one of the nation's most recognized trial advocacy programs. During his academic career, he earned numerous "best advocate" awards while competing in nine select varsity law school advocacy tournaments under the guidance of the late Colonel Hillard, a JAG officer. His dedication to mock trial competition and stellar academic performance laid the foundation for his aggressive yet compassionate approach to criminal defense.

Alex practices in both state and federal courts, handling a wide range of criminal matters including murder, manslaughter, DWI/DUI, sexual assault, controlled substances, domestic violence, aggravated robbery, white collar crimes, and criminal appeals. He has successfully achieved dismissals and favorable reductions for numerous clients, with case results available on his firm's website. Selected to Super Lawyers Rising Stars for 2025 in Criminal Defense, Alex has built a reputation as a tireless advocate who prioritizes his clients' freedom and rights. Operating from his office at 917 Franklin Street in Houston, he serves clients with the same passion and commitment that defined his journey from award-winning law student to respected criminal defense attorney.

Bio:
Adam Sumii serves as Managing Partner of MediSource RCM, a revenue cycle management company headquartered in Colleyville, Texas. In this leadership role, he oversees the company's mission to help healthcare providers maximize reimbursement, increase collections, reduce expenses, and streamline workflows for independent medical practices.

Under Sumii's direction, MediSource RCM leverages over 20 years of combined medical experience in billing, coding, and practice management to provide transparent and dependable revenue cycle solutions. The company specializes exclusively in revenue cycle management services, offering focused expertise in navigating the ever-changing requirements for prompt claim payment and maximum reimbursement. Through rigorous data analysis, pattern tracking, and strategic planning, Sumii and his team provide independent healthcare practices with the specialized attention to detail necessary to optimize their financial operations.

Bio:
Matt Tamez, ACHE, CPCO, CPB, serves as Managing Partner at Phoenix RCM, a revenue cycle management firm based in Rockwall, Texas, specializing in out-of-network billing and emergency medicine revenue cycle management. A U.S. Army veteran who served as a Combat Medic, Matt brings more than 30 years of leadership experience spanning both healthcare and technology sectors. Prior to co-founding Phoenix RCM (formerly Phoenix Coding and Consulting) in 2016 with Terri Tamez, he spent 24 years in IT management, leading complex teams for organizations ranging from small start-ups to Fortune 100 companies.

At Phoenix RCM, Matt applies his expertise in technology and business process management to help emergency medicine providers and facilities maximize reimbursement through expert NSA and TDI IDR support. He is actively involved in advocacy efforts with trade organizations for emergency room physicians and freestanding emergency centers at both state and federal levels. Matt is a frequent speaker on healthcare topics and contributes to the company's strategic direction, combining compliance, technology-driven solutions, and over 25 years of coding expertise to protect revenue and simplify the reimbursement process for healthcare providers.

Company Description:
Massachusetts General Hospital is home to the largest hospital-based research enterprise in the U.S., with an annual budget of over $912 million in 2017. The Mass General Research Institute comprises more than 8,500 researchers working across more than 30 institutes, centers and departments.
As the oldest and largest teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School, Mass General has been a leader in bridging innovative science with highly advanced clinical care for more than 200 years.
The Mass General Research Institute is built on a culture of excellence, compassion and groundbreaking scientific achievement. Our researchers work side-by-side with physicians—in the lab, in the clinic and at the bedside—harnessing the latest technological advancements to foster discovery at every stage.
Among the medical and scientific “firsts” at Mass General are the first demonstration of ether as a general anesthetic, the first x-ray exposure produced in a hospital, the first antiseptic operating suite in the U.S., and the invention of MRI and fMRI imaging technologies. [More Facts at a Glance]
Our location at the nexus of the Boston-Cambridge biotech cluster creates numerous opportunities to collaborate with partners from industry, venture capital, philanthropists, academia, foundations, governments, philanthropists and our community to prevent disease, make medical innovation sustainable, and find cures to improve the lives of our patients and those across the globe.
The Research Institute Steering Committee is focused on establishing broader relationships with these potential partners, and finding new ways to connect them with our highly knowledgeable scientists and clinicians.

Company Description:
Western Dental & Orthodontics (including affiliates such as Brident & Vital Smiles) is one of the nation’s largest dental providers and the leader in accessible, affordable oral healthcare.  With 350+ affiliated offices throughout CA, AL, TX, AZ, and NV, our network includes over 1200 dentists in which over 300 of them are Board Specialists.  In California, we are the leading provider of services to both adults and children in the Medi-Cal Dental public insurance program that provides health care benefits for more than 13 million low-income individuals and families.
In addition to general dentistry, Western Dental offers orthodontics, oral surgery, pediatric dentistry, periodontics and endodontics. All services are backed by a unique quality assurance system that electronically monitors all patient visits, treatments and clinical performance to enable high-quality care.
Our Corporate Offices based in Orange, CA provide support to affiliated offices throughout various departments including: Operations, Human Resources, Marketing, Procurement, IT, Facilities, Revenue Cycle Management, Accounting, Quality Management, and more.
We’ve been making quality care more affordable since our founder, Dr. Curtis Beauchamp, opened his first office in 1903.  A true visionary, he opened up dentistry to the people and championed the idea that everyone should have good oral health.  He did it by delivering excellent care at affordable prices and letting his patients pay over time. Back then, they called it "Credit Dentistry.”  These days, it's how almost all of us afford the braces, root canals and implants we need.  By the 1930’s Dr. Beauchamp and his son, Dr. Robert F. Beauchamp, were treating thousands of patients at multiple locations.  Fast forward to today, and we serve over 1 million people a year in five states.  As a true Dental Home, we treat patients of all ages through our general and multi-specialty platform.

Title: Revenue Cycle Manager
Company Location: 5 Independence Way Suite 300, Princeton, NJ 08540, United States
Company Description:
Expeditive is the nation’s #1 provider of interim staffing and consulting for Patient Financial Services and revenue cycle management.

We created this category because we saw the need hospitals have for experienced temporary staff and consultants who can handle the challenges of revenue cycle management. We remain the industry leader because of our ability to react quickly and provide our clients with an immediate solution.

Whether your hospital requires a complete turnaround in the billing department or you simply need to fill an unexpected vacancy, Expeditive’s experts can be on premise within 48 hours. We are known for sending A/R swat teams to hospitals to drive down aging receivables.

What separates us from other healthcare staffers is that we work onsite with hospital staff and their current systems rather than providing outsourced services. This enables us to understand the source of your revenue cycle problems and address them directly. Our interim billing consultants average 13+ years of hospital A/R resolution experience and are uniquely qualified to get your revenue cycle management back on track.

Expeditive’s experts are always ready to help a Patient Financial Services department in need of a turnaround. To learn more and get in contact with us, visit our website, Expeditive.com.

You can also connect with us on Twitter @Expeditive!

NExTT is used as injury tracking software in the pro and college athletic space, and as practice management in the health center and counseling space.  
Vivature serves over 200 campus health centers, athletic departments, high schools, and pro teams across the country.

Title: Managing Partner
Company Location: 11807 Westheimer Rd #550, Houston, TX 77077, United States
Company Description:
AMHA is a company that specializes in assisting patients who elect to receive “Alternative, Integrated” medical care at facilities throughout the U.S and internationally. Traditionally these providers require their patients to pay cash up-front for their medical care. Many practices offer some form of a receipt or bill to their patients but do not offer any assistance as it pertains to the preparation and submission of the bills to the patient's insurance company.  The majority of patients have little to no experience when dealing with their insurance companies and the complex issues involved with claims processing.  Therefore just supplying a master bill does not always overcome the challenges placed by insurance companies. Considering the cost associated with any medical treatment you want the best opportunity at receiving a reimbursement.  

When offering perspective patients the option of reimbursements (from their insurance company) patients will find it easier choosing to visit a facility knowing that there is a chance of an insurance reimbursement. This helps reduce the financial stress for those patients and allows them to concentrate on their travel and treatment. All of AMHA's current clients do not accept insurance and they expect payment up-front for the services provided. AMHA's service cost nothing to the practices we represent, providers of care only refer AMHA's services to their patients and AMHA deals directly with patients to handle the billing. If AMHA succeeds, then patients can afford to return for additional treatments and therefore both the patients and the practice benefit from our service. As a third-party patient advocate company AMHA takes you facility practice out of the equation. AMHA's service has proven to be a boom for the practices we represent in both added traffic from new and returning patients!

Title: Intraoperative Neurophysiologist
Company Location: 1819 Jay Ell Drive, Richardson, TX 75081, United States
Company Description:
Axis Neuromonitoring provides high-quality intraoperative neurophysiological monitoring (IONM) services across Texas, ensuring optimal surgical outcomes and patient safety.
Our expert team utilizes advanced technology to monitor nerve integrity and neurological responses during surgical procedures. With an onsite AXIS technologist and offsite telemonitoring physician, we deliver real-time data to assist surgeons in preserving nerve function and preventing complications. This critical support enhances surgical precision and helps reduce the risk of neurological injury.
At XIS Neuromonitoring, we prioritize expertise and professionalism. All of our staff are Board Certified or Board Eligible by ABRET, with certifications in CNIM (Certified Neurophysiologic Intraoperative Monitoring) or EPT (Evoked Potential Technologist).
We are committed to providing reliable, accurate, and timely neurophysiological monitoring services to healthcare providers, ensuring the safety of every patient throughout their surgical journey.

With an onsite AXIS technologist and offsite telemonitoring physician, we deliver real-time data to assist surgeons in preserving nerve function and preventing complications. This critical support enhances surgical precision and helps reduce the risk of neurological injury.
At XIS Neuromonitoring, we prioritize expertise and professionalism. All of our staff are Board Certified or Board Eligible by ABRET, with certifications in CNIM (Certified Neurophysiologic Intraoperative Monitoring) or EPT (Evoked Potential Technologist).
We are committed to providing reliable, accurate, and timely neurophysiological monitoring services to healthcare providers, ensuring the safety of every patient throughout their surgical journey.

Title: plastic surgeon
Company Location: 4316 James Casey Street Bldg F, Austin, TX 78745, United States
Company Description:
Austin Oculofacial Plastics and now Austin Face and Body was founded by Dr. Sean Paul with the goal to provide outstanding care to the Austin area in the practice of eyelid and facial plastic surgery.

Dr. Paul grew up in south Texas. He was accepted into the highly prestigious Business Honors Program and was named Student of the Year by the Cactus Yearbook and awarded the Outstanding Business Student of the Year of Award during his time at The University of Texas at Austin.  He stayed in Texas for medical school, where he served as Class President at The University of Texas Medical School in San Antonio.  Next, he was accepted to the highly regarded Dean McGee Eye Institute for ophthalmology residency. After residency, Dr. Paul was chosen from a highly competitive field of applicants to train for two years as an American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS) Fellow under the mentorship of Dr. Gerald Harris, world renowned Orbital and Oculofacial surgeon and past editor of Ophthalmic Plastic Reconstructive Surgery, at the prestigious Medical College of Wisconsin in Milwaukee. He pursued elective training in cosmetic surgery of the eyelids and face in Miami, Beverly Hills, and the greater Chicago area and is thrilled to return home to Austin, Texas.

Dr. Paul is a board certified physician and surgeon by the American Board of Ophthalmology (ABO) and a candidate member of the American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS). Dr. Paul specializes in eyelid and facial plastic (cosmetic and reconstructive) surgery. He uses a blended approach of non-surgical treatments and surgery, including minimal incision techniques, to provide the best patient care.  Dr. Paul has authored numerous scientific articles and has been invited to speak at many national and international meetings including Lake Como, Italy and Sydney, Australia. He has returned home to Texas and founded Austin Oculofacial Plastics in Austin, Texas.

Title: plastic surgeon
Company Location: 6110 Sherry Lane, Dallas, TX 75225, United States
Company Description:
Dr. George A. Toledo graduated from the University of Texas in Austin with high honors and a Phi Beta Kappa. He attended Southwestern Medical School in Dallas, graduating in 1980 at the top of his class.

After graduation he spent seven years of post-graduate surgical training consisting of internships and residencies at the following institutions: Baylor University Medical Center, Duke University Medical Center, Louisiana State University Medical Center, and the many hospitals that make up the Texas Medical Center in Houston. While in Houston, Dr. Toledo had the opportunity to train at the prestigious University of Texas M.D. Anderson Cancer Institute.

Board Certified by the American Board of Plastic Surgery as well as the American Board of Otolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery, provides Dr. Toledo added expertise in facial surgery, especially cosmetic and reconstructive surgery of the face and nose. He is a Fellow of the American College of Surgeons and the American Society of Plastic Surgeons as well as a member of many other medical and surgical societies.

Since 1985, Dr. Toledo’s practice has evolved from reconstructive surgery to primarily cosmetic and aesthetic procedures. Today the focus is three fold: the aging face and eyes, breast surgery and body contouring, and rhinoplasties. Dr. Toledo has developed a reputation for treating difficult complications arising from cosmetic surgery throughout the United States and Latin America.

Title: plastic surgeon
Company Location: 4316 James Casey Street Bldg F, Austin, TX 78745, United States
Company Description:
Austin Oculofacial Plastics and now Austin Face and Body was founded by Dr. Sean Paul with the goal to provide outstanding care to the Austin area in the practice of eyelid and facial plastic surgery.

Dr. Paul grew up in south Texas. He was accepted into the highly prestigious Business Honors Program and was named Student of the Year by the Cactus Yearbook and awarded the Outstanding Business Student of the Year of Award during his time at The University of Texas at Austin.  He stayed in Texas for medical school, where he served as Class President at The University of Texas Medical School in San Antonio.  Next, he was accepted to the highly regarded Dean McGee Eye Institute for ophthalmology residency. After residency, Dr. Paul was chosen from a highly competitive field of applicants to train for two years as an American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS) Fellow under the mentorship of Dr. Gerald Harris, world renowned Orbital and Oculofacial surgeon and past editor of Ophthalmic Plastic Reconstructive Surgery, at the prestigious Medical College of Wisconsin in Milwaukee. He pursued elective training in cosmetic surgery of the eyelids and face in Miami, Beverly Hills, and the greater Chicago area and is thrilled to return home to Austin, Texas.

Dr. Paul is a board certified physician and surgeon by the American Board of Ophthalmology (ABO) and a candidate member of the American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS). Dr. Paul specializes in eyelid and facial plastic (cosmetic and reconstructive) surgery. He uses a blended approach of non-surgical treatments and surgery, including minimal incision techniques, to provide the best patient care.  Dr. Paul has authored numerous scientific articles and has been invited to speak at many national and international meetings including Lake Como, Italy and Sydney, Australia. He has returned home to Texas and founded Austin Oculofacial Plastics in Austin, Texas.

Company Location: 1101 Matlock Road, Mansfield, TX 76063, United States
Company Description:
Dr. Bishara's special interests include cosmetic surgery of the breast (including augmentation and breast lift), rhinoplasty, abdominoplasty (tummy-tuck), liposuction, cosmetic surgery of the face, autologous fat transfer, and body contouring surgery after massive weight loss.

Dr. Bishara also provides a full array of minimally invasive cosmetic procedures, such as injectable fillers, Botox, and hair restoration including transplantation. He offers the latest technique in hair transplantation, known as follicular unit extraction (FUE) - a bloodless procedure that is essentially pain-free and does not involve the traditional incision of the scalp.


Dr. Bishara then completed a five-year residency in general surgery at Morristown Memorial hospital, which is affiliated with the prestigious Mount Sinai Medical School in Manhattan, New York. During this time he founded the American Plastic and Reconstructive Research Association - an industrial think-tank for novel concepts and respective clinical applications in plastic surgery. He then completed a fellowship appointment in Aesthetic and Reconstructive Plastic Surgery at Harvard Medical School at the world-renowned Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center in Boston, Massachusetts.

Title: Spine Surgeon
Company Location: 4000 Church Road, Mount Laurel, NJ 08054, United States
Company Description:
A multidisciplinary spine center focused on nonsurgical solutions to back and neck pain

Coastal Spine is a multidisciplinary spine center focused on nonsurgical solutions to back and neck pain. We are a regional Center of Excellence, working with patients from around the region on simple to complex cases.  Our goal is to end chronic pain, and return our patients back to normal activity and health as soon as possible.

We Offer:
A non-surgical approach to resolving back and neck pain
On-site physical therapy
On-site nonsurgical procedures, including injections
On-site diagnostic testing, including x-rays and EMG’s.
In cases where surgery is necessary, our spine surgeons perform minimally invasive surgery at our nearby dedicated spine surgery center or local hospital.

What Makes Us Unique?
An Emphasis on a Nonsurgical Approach
We place emphasis on a nonsurgical approach to back and neck problems. While surgery may be appropriate in some cases, we explore all nonsurgical options first so that you can get back to being pain free as soon as possible.

Everything Under One Roof
We put everything under one roof. No more shuttling back and forth between doctor's offices. We are one of the only spine centers in New Jersey to combine spine-specialized orthopedic surgeons and spine neurosurgeons together with specialists in nonsurgical disciplines for treatment of back and neck pain.

Dedicated to Treating Spine Conditions
Nowhere else in the region will you find a team of physicians and staff who are dedicated solely to treating spine conditions that affect the neck and back. Learn more about our doctors.

We are a highly specialized practice. We see patients who have experienced back and neck pain for the first time and patients who have tried other types of treatment without relief. Whatever your condition, we will get you back to normal activity as smoothly and as soon as possible.

Title: Senior Counsel
Company Location: 500 Plaza Drive, Secaucus, NJ 07094, United States
Company Description:
Quest Diagnostics (NYSE: DGX) empowers people to take action to improve health outcomes.  Derived from the world's largest database of clinical lab results, our diagnostic insights reveal new avenues to identify and treat disease, inspire healthy behaviors and improve health care management.  Quest annually serves one in three adult Americans and half the physicians and hospitals in the United States, and our 47,000 employees understand that, in the right hands and with the right context, our diagnostic insights can inspire actions that transform lives.
The company offers physicians the broadest test menu (3,000+ tests), is a pioneer in developing innovative new tests, is the leader in cancer diagnostics, provides anatomic pathology (AP) services, & interpretive consultation through its medical & scientific staff of about 900 M.D.s & Ph.D.s. The company reported 2020 revenues of $9.44 billion. 
Quest Diagnostics offers the most extensive clinical testing network in the U.S., with laboratories in most major metropolitan areas, & in Mexico, the UK & India. The company also operates four esoteric laboratories, 40 outpatient AP laboratories, & 160 smaller, rapid-response laboratories. 
Patients may have specimens collected in any of the company’s approximately 2,250 patient service centers. On a typical workday, testing is performed for about 550,000 patients. 
Quest Diagnostics empowers healthcare organizations & clinicians with state-of-the-art connectivity solutions. 
The company is the leading provider of pre-employment drugs-of-abuse screening for employers & risk assessment services for the life insurance industry. It is the world’s 2nd largest provider of clinical trials testing for new pharmaceuticals.
More information is available at www.questdiagnostics.com.

Company Description:
One of the nation’s preeminent plaintiffs’ law firms, Seeger Weiss is best known for multidistrict mass torts and class actions in both state and federal court. From offices in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Massachusetts, the firm has represented over 10,000 individuals, companies, and governments across the U.S. who have been injured or defrauded on a massive scale.
Since its founding in 1999, it has led many of the most complex and high-profile cases in the country: the National Prescription Opiate Litigation, which the Washington Post called “the largest federal court case in U.S. history”; 3M Combat Arms Earplug Products Liability Litigation, which the Minneapolis Star Tribune called “one of the largest mass torts ever”; the ongoing “Dieselgate” scandal; the sprawling multistate litigation on behalf of survivors of child sexual abuse; and the history-making Football League Players’ Concussion Injury Litigation.
Unlike many plaintiffs’ firms, Seeger Weiss doesn’t simply “collect” clients for other lawyers to serve. We go to court and get results, regularly winning amounts in hundreds of millions and billions of dollars on behalf of large classes of injured clients. Whatever the case, we have the experience and resources necessary to go toe-to-toe with the biggest and best-funded corporations in America.
We are proud to share the Seeger Weiss Diversity Fellowship as a part of our ongoing commitment to recruiting, retaining, and promoting attorneys from underrepresented backgrounds.

Title: Director of Operations, Class Actions Division
Company Location: 1 Holtec Dr #100, Marlton, NJ 08053, United States
Company Description:
Console & Associates is a leading class action law firm specializing in data breach, consumer privacy, antitrust, and securities litigation. Founded in 1994, we represent plaintiffs nationwide in complex class action matters, with particular expertise in cases involving corporate data security failures and privacy violations.
Rick Console, our Founder, Attorney, and Principal, has dedicated his 25-year legal career to consumer advocacy, successfully challenging some of America's largest corporations and securing over $100 million in compensation for more than 7,000 clients. His work has been featured in Forbes and The Washington Post.
Our data breach practice has become increasingly vital as cyber threats evolve. Recent years have seen unprecedented levels of corporate data breaches, with stolen personal information often sold on dark web marketplaces, leading to identity theft, fraudulent loans, and unemployment fraud. We hold companies accountable when they fail to implement adequate safeguards for consumer data, helping victims secure compensation for their losses.
Beyond data security matters, our class action practice extends to:
 • Consumer Privacy Protection
 • Antitrust Violations
 • Securities Fraud
 • Corporate Misconduct
 • Consumer Protection
In today's digital age, the impact of corporate data breaches extends far beyond immediate financial losses. When stolen personal information is sold on the dark web, it often leads to cascading damage through identity theft, fraudulent loans, and unemployment claims. Companies entrusted with sensitive personal information have a legal duty to protect it, and when they fail to do so, they must be held accountable.
Our firm has the expertise and resources to help you pursue compensation from parties responsible for compromising your data security. Contact us at 856-778-5500 for a free case evaluation to learn more about your rights and potential legal remedies.
